source.title: Rewarded Outcomes Enhance Reactivation of Experience in the Hippocampus

confidence_rationale: Strong empirical evidence that reward receipt enhances SWR reactivation, especially during learning when reward is novel/surprising. Direct electrophysiology in hippocampal CA3. Mapping gap: the study measures reward salience, not prediction error per se -- reward and surprise are confounded during learning phases. The effect is on reactivation frequency, not on whether replay is generative.
failure_signatures: Reward and surprise are confounded during learning phases -- the study cannot disambiguate whether enhanced reactivation is driven by reward value or by prediction error specifically.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Receipt of reward enhances hippocampal SWR reactivation of associated spatial sequences, and this enhancement is strongest during learning of new path-reward associations.
mapping.ree_translation: Maps onto MECH-205's surprise-gating: salient outcomes (reward, especially novel reward during learning) increase replay priority for the associated experience. The learning-phase enhancement suggests prediction error (reward prediction error specifically) drives the prioritisation, since reward is most surprising when the association is new.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The study measures reward-modulated reactivation, not surprise-modulated reactivation directly. MECH-205 claims replay is gated by prediction error across all domains (sensory, motor, harm/goal), not just reward. The study does not address whether replay is generative (variations) or rote (re-presentation).
mapping.source_context: Rodent (rat) hippocampal CA3 single-unit recordings during spatial sequence-switching task with explicit reward/no-reward comparison.

confidence_components.notes: Neuron, well-controlled experiment isolating reward from trajectory. Mapping fidelity reduced because the study tests reward salience, not prediction error directly, and does not address the generative aspect. Transfer risk moderate: rodent spatial navigation to REE's general architecture.
